name: Bass Pro Shops & Cabela's CLUB Card

verdict.text: Only earns its keep for frequent Bass Pro Shops / Cabela's shoppers who can climb the spend tiers — the 1% base rate elsewhere is weak, and CLUB Points are a closed-loop currency good only for merchandise at those two chains (plus Mack's Prairie Wings). Skip it if you shop there occasionally rather than as your outdoor-gear default.

earn_rates.category: Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's & participating Cenex — CLUB Classic (default tier)
Bass Pro Shops & Cabela's — CLUB Silver, after $10,000 in annual spend
Bass Pro Shops & Cabela's — CLUB Black, after $25,000 in annual spend

earn_note: One card issued and marketed under two brand names since Bass Pro Shops acquired Cabela's — Capital One's Bass Pro Shops CLUB page and Cabela's CLUB page describe identical terms: same $0 fee, same tiered 2%/3%/5% rates at either chain, same welcome offer, same Mastercard network. Every account is auto-enrolled at CLUB Classic and climbs to Silver/Black based on trailing annual spend, with no separate application needed. Cardholders also get 9.99% APR financing on Bass Pro/Cabela's purchases and a 5% price-beat guarantee against competitors' listed prices.

redemption_options.method: Merchandise, gear & experiences at Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's & Mack's Prairie Wings (100 points = $1)

points.note: CLUB Points never expire on an open account and there's no earning cap, but there's no cash-back or statement-credit option — every point has to be spent back into the Bass Pro/Cabela's ecosystem, including big-ticket redemptions like boats and ATVs.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners — CLUB Points are a closed-loop merchandise currency, not Capital One miles or cash back.

insurance.detail: Not listed in Capital One's own Guide to Benefits for this card
Not listed in Capital One's own Guide to Benefits for this card
Not listed in Capital One's own Guide to Benefits for this card
Not listed in Capital One's own Guide to Benefits for this card
Dark-web/credit monitoring and resolution support, enrollment required

protection_note: Thinner than most co-brand cards: Capital One's official Guide to Benefits for the Cabela's/Bass Pro CLUB Mastercard covers only Mastercard ID Theft Protection, a Concierge Service, Mastercard Travel & Lifestyle Services (hotel rate/stay guarantees via a third-party lifestyle manager), and Airport Concierge discounts — it does not list purchase protection, extended warranty, price protection, travel accident insurance, or rental car coverage, despite some third-party reviews implying otherwise. Don't assume standard Mastercard World Elite purchase insurance carries over here without confirming your specific cardholder agreement.
rental_note: No rental car coverage is listed in Capital One's Guide to Benefits for this card — use a different card for rental cars.

services.name: 9.99% APR on Bass Pro/Cabela's purchases
services.detail: A reduced promotional rate specifically for purchases at either chain; standard variable APR applies elsewhere.

services.name: 5% price-beat guarantee
services.detail: CLUB members get a price match plus 5% against competitors on eligible items at Bass Pro Shops and Cabela's.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: $0 on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: Mastercard Concierge & Travel Services
services.detail: 24/7 concierge line, hotel rate/stay guarantees through a third-party lifestyle manager, and Airport Concierge meet-and-greet discounts.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Their spend counts toward the account's tier thresholds
No purchase protection to share — this card doesn't advertise any

additional_cards.note: Free to add, and useful for households that share Bass Pro/Cabela's shopping, since an authorized user's spend counts toward the $10,000/$25,000 tier thresholds.

timeline.text: Following Bass Pro Shops' 2017 acquisition of Cabela's, the two loyalty programs were combined onto a single Capital One-issued Mastercard, replacing the old Cabela's CLUB Visa that had been issued by Cabela's own World's Foremost Bank.
